notes: With-skill achieves 100% pass rate across all 5 evals — perfect score. The three structural rules (Voss label, no call in DM 2, no pitch in DM 2) are the clearest differentiators.

Without-skill highest at Eval 5 (peer/agency owner, 80%) — natural peer framing is accessible without the skill. The Voss label incidentally appeared ('It sounds like') but the build-side calibrated question and peer-track phase label were absent.

Without-skill lowest at Eval 4 (pain disclosed, 40%) — the phase-skipping behavior (Label -> Proof, dropping a case study and call ask immediately) is the most common natural mistake and the skill prevents it cleanly.
The 'circling back' failure in Eval 2 without-skill is a concrete example of banned language the skill explicitly blacklists — validates that assertion.
No assertions appear non-discriminating: every assertion that passes in without-skill also fails at least once, and vice versa. The eval set has good discriminating power.
Scenario A evals (DM sequence) show larger deltas (+0.333, +0.30) than Scenario B peer track (+0.20) — the sequence rules are more constraining and harder to follow without the reference structure.
